title: Today's Markets Brief: Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market
summary: A look at the latest market trends and why double-digit earnings growth may not be enough to prevent the next bear market.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market
imageCaption: Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market
imageSourceTitle: Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market

body.text: First up, we have a piece from MarketWatch that caught my eye: 'Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market.' Now, you'd think that double-digit earnings growth would be a good thing, and it usually is. But apparently, history suggests that when the S&P 500 profits start spiking like this, it often signals the final innings of a bull market. Yeah, it's like the market is saying, 'Hey, let's make some money, but also, let's get ready for a nap.' It's a bit counterintuitive, but that's what makes it so interesting.

Next, I came across another article from MarketWatch titled 'This bear market signal Wall Street ignores is putting your money at risk right now.' Veteran trader Steve Burns is sounding the alarm, preparing for a 50% decline and pointing out three 'deadly stock-market sins' that are destroying portfolios. I guess you could say he's not exactly the life of the party, but someone's got to bring the cautionary tale. It's always good to hear from experienced traders like Steve, even if their outlook isn't exactly sunny.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
